data.title: As the sprint orchestrator, I route tasks to the right test-writing behavior based on testing_mode
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the sprint orchestrator to consume the testing_mode field from implementation-plan task output and route to the appropriate test-writing behavior per mode.
**Why**: Without routing, all tasks are treated as RED (requiring new failing tests), even when the task only changes implementation or when existing tests already cover the behavior.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint orchestrator reads testing_mode from task output
- IN: RED mode → dispatch dso:red-test-writer as today
- IN: GREEN mode → skip RED test writing, validate existing tests pass after implementation
- IN: UPDATE mode → update existing tests to reflect new behavior, then implement
- IN: Backward compatibility — tasks without testing_mode default to current behavior (RED)
- OUT: Modifying implementation-plan output format (separate story)
- OUT: Modifying the shared testing standard itself

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the sprint orchestrator routes tasks to RED, GREEN, or UPDATE test-writing behavior based on the testing_mode field
  ← Satisfies: "The sprint orchestrator is updated to consume the testing_mode field from implementation-plan task output and route to the appropriate test-writing behavior per mode"
- When this story is complete, tasks without a testing_mode field are handled with backward-compatible default behavior
  ← Satisfies: "The sprint orchestrator is updated to consume the testing_mode field"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Backward compatibility with in-progress epics whose tasks lack the testing_mode field — must default gracefully

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
